---
name: working-notes
description: Disk-first working notes for terminal agents. Execution sequence: name artifact → read verifier → read all source  files → stub output → copy volatile resources → implement at 60% → fix failures literally → change structure after three consecutive same-shape failures → plan briefly then act. Spec literals are contracts (dates/paths/names/separators/quoting/trailing newlines). Measure transformations, don't predict them — inspect intermediate pipeline state directly. Search environment broadly for missing keys/credentials/parameters. Restore file readability before parsing. Validate service config syntax AND live connectivity before exit. Measure reference match quantitatively, prefer deterministic. Test cancellation cleanup explicitly per started  task. Inspect unknown programs cheapest-first. Fix smallest failing unit. Read disk   artifact before stopping.
---

# Working Notes

What you write to disk is what counts.

## Execution sequence

Name the artifact first — its exact path, its exact
format, its exact success condition. Then read the
verifier before writing a single line of solution.

Read all source files the task references. The actual
behavior of any component is in its source, not in
its name or its category. Read before predicting.

Stub the output file immediately with placeholder
content. Every subsequent step updates this  file.

Copy volatile resources before touching them. If a
resource maintains a transaction log, a journal, or
any form of state that may be modified by access,
copy all associated files to a working location
before issuing any command against them.

Implement at 60% confidence and save. Run the check.
Read failure output literally — the error message
names what is wrong. Fix that specific thing. Save
and re-run.

If the same structural approach fails three consecutive
times, the structure is wrong. Stop varying parameters.
Diagnose the root cause. Choose a different approach.

Plan briefly, then act. Planning without action is
the most common failure mode.

## Environment constraints

Shell, Python, file I/O. Standard library and
pre-installed packages only. No speculative imports.

## Treating specification values as contracts

Every literal value in  the task description is a
contract term: file paths, function names, date
values, output field names, separator characters,
quoting style.   Reproduce them without  modification.

Pinned dates must be written as literal values in
code. Tests compare  against the  pinned value, not
against whatever the clock returns at runtime.

When sample output is provided, match it exactly   —
every character, every separator, every trailing
newline.

##   Ground truth over prediction

When a task involves components that transform data,
measure the transformation rather than predicting it.
Run the component on your input. Inspect the output.
That output is ground truth. Your prediction  of what
the component should produce is not.

This applies to any pipeline where one component's
output becomes another component's input. Inspect
the intermediate state directly.

## Searching the environment

When recovery or configuration requires information
not explicitly provided — a key, a credential, a
parameter — search the environment before concluding
it is absent. Relevant information may appear in
configuration files, environment variables, source
files, binary strings, comments, or documentation
in the working directory and its   parents.

## Data recovery

When a file cannot be opened by its native tool,
the path forward is to restore readability, not to
parse the binary  format manually. Identify what
prevents the tool from reading the file. Find the
information needed to remove that obstacle. Apply
it. Then use the native tool.

## Service configuration

Configuration scripts must leave services in a
verifiably running state. Validate configuration
syntax before starting. After starting, confirm
the service is accepting connections. Do not exit
the script until this is confirmed.

All literal values in configuration — format strings,
directive names, port numbers, option syntax — are
tested exactly. Match the specification.

## Quantitative output matching

When producing output that must match a reference,
measure the match before finalizing. Sample the
reference systematically.  Derive parameters from
measurement. Verify the metric. Prefer deterministic
generation when time and size are constrained.

## Concurrent execution

When implementing concurrent work with cancellation,
the required property is that cleanup runs for every
task that started. Verify this by testing: start
tasks, trigger cancellation, confirm cleanup ran for
each started task.

## Investigating unknown programs

Cheapest first: inspect file type, byte content,
embedded strings. Request usage information. Run
with minimal input. Observe all output channels.
Escalate to system call tracing when needed.

## Code discipline

Fix the smallest unit that resolves the failure.
Validate  at trust boundaries. Use stable time
sources. Comments only for non-obvious reasoning.

## Exit condition

Read the artifact on disk before stopping. Update
it if it diverges from your current understanding.
The disk state is the answer.
